methodology: Scoring of market opportunities based on pain intensity, market size, competitive white space, and SF2 alignment

OPPORTUNITY_1_SLICE_FIX.description: Position SF2 as the definitive slice-fixing driver that actually works

pain_quotes: P-021: if I swing as hard as I can, I could probably send the ball 2 fairways to the right
P-041: Im almost to the point where I think I'll never be able to get rid of my slice
transformation_quotes: H-160: WOW I never thought i'd lose my slice but its gone. My ball flies very straight about 85% of the time now
H-145: I rarely lose a drive right these days

go_to_market_implications.primary_message: Finally fix your slice - for good
go_to_market_implications.risk_to_address: Skepticism that equipment can fix swing

OPPORTUNITY_2_DISTANCE_PLUS_ACCURACY.description: Position SF2 as the ONLY solution that fixes slice without losing distance

pain_quotes: V-124: 10-15 yds. is a LOT, and there is just no way that hitting a club or two more into 14 greens is a good long-term recipe
P-250: a new PXG gen4 XF I have is only getting 240 or so yards. I can do 235 with my 15 degree 3 wood
desire_quotes: H-172: On average I lost 10-15 yards but that's if I hit my driver perfect
V-127: I think you need to find a way to maximize length while minimizing risk

go_to_market_implications.primary_message: Why choose? Get distance AND fairways
go_to_market_implications.competitive_angle: Unlike draw bias drivers that sacrifice distance

OPPORTUNITY_3_CONFIDENCE_RESTORATION.description: Position SF2 as the path back to trusting your driver

pain_quotes: P-046: mentally I am prepared to hit a bad shot before I even step up to the ball
P-221: I have lost all ability to hit the driver or any woods
transformation_quotes: H-173: I get in a lot less trouble with my 3w than with my driver and that makes my round a lot more fun

go_to_market_implications.primary_message: Step up to the tee without dread

OPPORTUNITY_4_SENIOR_DISTANCE_RECOVERY.description: Position SF2 for seniors losing distance to age

pain_quotes: P-241: 250 + yard drives was the norm for me. distance was gone, I mean G-O-N-E gone
P-231: Each year as I get older, I lose 10 yards per year
transformation_quotes: H-154: For a 70+ yr old, that's a real game changer
H-169: distance - considerably longer and frankly, it was intoxicating

go_to_market_implications.primary_message: Get back the distance time took from you

OPPORTUNITY_5_EMBARRASSMENT_ELIMINATION.description: Position SF2 as the social redemption solution

pain_quotes: P-251: I make a fool of myself with 460 CC head
P-226: The guys I was playing with tried to hide their frustrations with my 200 yard tee shot but I could sense it

go_to_market_implications.primary_message: Stop being the short hitter in your group

tier_1_must_execute.rationale: Both scored 44, and together they form the unique SF2 value proposition
tier_1_must_execute.combined_message: Fix your slice without sacrificing distance

tier_2_strong_support.rationale: Powerful emotional hook that differentiates from technical competitors

tier_3_segment_specific.rationale: Large segment with high lifetime value, but requires specific targeting
Strong emotional content but smaller market

messaging_hierarchy.primary_promise: Fix your slice without sacrificing distance
supporting_claims: 85% of golfers hit straighter in first round
Average 15+ yards more than current driver
emotional_hooks: Finally enjoy the first tee again

competitive_positioning_statement.for: Golfers who have tried everything to fix their slice
competitive_positioning_statement.who_are: Frustrated with wasting money on lessons and equipment that don't work
competitive_positioning_statement.sf2_is: The first driver engineered specifically to turn your slice into straight shots
competitive_positioning_statement.that_delivers: Fairways AND distance - not one or the other
competitive_positioning_statement.unlike: Draw bias drivers that make you choose between accuracy and distance
competitive_positioning_statement.because: SF2's unique [mechanism] works WITH your natural swing instead of fighting against it
